verses.text: The LORD: when ye come into the land I give you and offer a burnt offering, bring with the lamb a tenth of flour with oil and a fourth of wine. For a ram, two tenths and a third of wine; for a bullock, three tenths and half a hin. One law for the homeborn and the stranger. Guidance: God speaks of the land while they still wander — He plans for their arrival, not their drift. The stranger gets the same access to worship as the native-born. No second-tier worshippers in His house.

The LORD: when ye eat of the bread of the land, offer up an heave offering — a cake of the first of your dough — unto the LORD. Of the first of your dough ye shall give the LORD an heave offering throughout your generations. Guidance: Every loaf says thank you before the family says it. The first cake from the new harvest goes up; the rest comes down to the table. God's portion is small — but it must be FIRST.

If ye have erred and not observed all these commandments, the congregation shall offer one bullock for a burnt offering and one kid for a sin offering. It shall be forgiven them — for it was ignorance. If one soul sin through ignorance, he shall bring a she-goat. One law for homeborn and stranger. Guidance: God writes a way back for the heart that missed it. Ignorance is real and deserves a real sacrifice — but it does not exile. The door home stays open for the unintending.

The soul that doeth ought presumptuously, whether homeborn or stranger, the same reproacheth the LORD; that soul shall be cut off. He hath despised the word of the LORD and broken His commandment — that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity shall be upon him. Guidance: Presumption is sin with eyes open. The difference is not the act — it's the heart that knew and did it anyway. God forgives ignorance; He severs defiance. The line runs through the will, not the deed.

The difference is not the act — it's the heart that knew and did it anyway. God forgives ignorance; He severs defiance. The line runs through the will, not the deed.

Israel in the wilderness found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath. They put him in ward — it was not declared what should be done. The LORD: the man shall surely be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him. They stoned him, and he died. Guidance: A bundle of sticks on the wrong day cost a man his life. The sabbath was the visible sign of whose people they were. God's word was new, the warning had been given, and presumption had a body count.

The LORD: make fringes in the borders of your garments, with a ribband of blue, that ye may look upon it and remember all My commandments — not seek after your own heart and your own eyes. I am the LORD your God who brought you out of Egypt to be your God. Guidance: A thread of blue at the hem turns clothes into a sermon. Every glance down is a reminder: I am not my own. The eye that wanders gets caught by a tassel before it drifts.
